rag_rules: Place guarantees relating visible inputs to outputs in component type annexes; avoid implementation-level paths in type scope.
Use only visible feature names in AGREE expressions; do not reference subcomponent ports directly in type-level contracts.
generic_rules: Express behavioral relations as guarantees linking visible inputs to visible outputs using scalar arithmetic.
Declare helper boolean conditions using eq declarations to encapsulate range or logic checks.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use 'guarantee "label": expression;' for output dependencies; always terminate with semicolon.
Use 'eq name : bool = expression;' to define boolean conditions from real or int comparisons.
anti_patterns: Do not use AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) as variables in AGREE expressions.
Do not reference subcomponent paths (e.g., Throttle.Throttle_Impl) in component type annexes.
